pulse: Markets closed Friday on a cautious note after Intel-led chip rally faded into geopolitical concern over stalled US-Iran ceasefire talks. Brent crude held above $105 with Iran collecting Hormuz tolls, gold hit a record $4,722, and five Magnificent Seven companies report earnings this week â€” the biggest market event of the quarter.

movers.name: INTC
movers.reason: Intel Q1 earnings beat with revenue $13.6B vs $12.4B expected; data center revenue up 22% â€” best single-day gain since October 1987

movers.name: NVDA
movers.reason: Nvidia retook $5 trillion market cap on semiconductor momentum following Intel earnings catalyst

movers.name: AMD
movers.reason: AMD surged on earnings optimism and semiconductor sector rotation fueled by Intel beat

movers.name: QCOM
movers.reason: Qualcomm rallied in sympathy with broader chip sector strength post-Intel results

stocks: Lendlease REIT â€” monitors SGX session Monday
Monitor US open Monday for energy sector moves vs Brent $105+
portfolio: Marc's SGD-base portfolio faces a mixed backdrop. Gold at $4,722 is a clear winner at record levels. BTC at $77,382 is consolidating near key support. STI at ~5,003 has been resilient (+30% YoY) and MAS tightening supports SGD strength. IBEX35 pulled back 1.4% Friday but remains up 32% YoY. US positions benefit from chip rally but face a heavy Mag-7 earnings week as a binary catalyst April 29-30. Brent above $105 from Hormuz closure continues to pressure Singapore energy import costs. Watch EUR/SGD and USD/SGD on Monday open.

alerts.text: Hormuz strait: Iran collecting tolls on oil tankers; WTI above $106, Brent $105. Any escalation spikes oil and hits global growth.
EARNINGS WEEK: Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft, Meta, Apple all report April 29-30. Over 200 S&P 500 companies this week â€” single biggest binary risk event of Q1 season.
US-Iran ceasefire talks stalled after Trump rejected meeting with Iran FM Araghchi on April 25. Ceasefire extension intact but fragile.
IMF April 2026 WEO: global growth cut to 3.1% for 2026, down 0.5pp from February. Emerging markets cut to 3.9%.
ECB may hike in June 2026 â€” markets now pricing this after inflation forecast raised to 2.6%. Watch EUR/USD holding above 1.17.
Intel posted best single-day gain since October 1987. Semiconductor sector on 16+ consecutive positive days.
Singapore Q1 GDP: +4.6% YoY. MAS tightened SGD NEER slope on April 14. Supports SGD strength vs basket.

news.title: US-Iran ceasefire talks collapse as Trump rejects Araghchi meeting
news.text: Iran's FM Abbas Araghchi arrived in Islamabad on April 25 but the White House cancelled the US delegation after Trump said we have all the cards and they can call us anytime. The ceasefire remains technically extended but direct negotiations have stalled over Iran's preconditions: lift the US naval blockade and preserve the Lebanon ceasefire first.

news.title: Iran begins collecting Hormuz tolls â€” up to $20M daily from oil tankers
news.text: Iran collected its first toll revenue from ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, with analysts estimating potential daily revenue of up to $20 million from oil tankers alone. The closure continues to restrict the 27 percent of global maritime petroleum trade that passes through the strait.

news.title: Intel historic 24 percent surge anchors chip rally â€” best day since 1987
news.text: Intel Q1 results showed revenue of $13.6 billion versus $12.4 billion expected, with data center revenue up 22 percent to $5.1 billion. The 24 percent single-session gain was Intel's largest since October 1987, dragging Nvidia, AMD, and Qualcomm sharply higher in one of the strongest semiconductor sessions on record.

news.title: EU approves 106 billion euro loan package for Ukraine
news.text: The European Union formally approved a 106 billion euro two-year loan package covering both economic and military support for Ukraine, ending months of political deadlock among member states and providing funding certainty through at least 2028.

news.title: IMF cuts 2026 global growth forecast to 3.1 percent â€” war cited as primary drag
news.text: The IMF April 2026 World Economic Outlook cut global growth projections by 0.5 percentage points to 3.1 percent for 2026, with the Middle East conflict and Hormuz closure identified as the primary downside risks. Emerging market growth was cut from 4.2 to 3.9 percent.

news.title: Magnificent Seven earnings avalanche begins April 29
news.text: Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft, Meta, and Apple are all scheduled to report Q1 2026 earnings on April 29 and 30. Over 200 S&P 500 companies â€” 42 percent of the index â€” report this week. Early results show 82 percent of the first 134 reporters beating analyst estimates.

news.title: X-Energy IPO raises $1.02 billion for Amazon-backed nuclear firm
news.text: X-Energy raised $1.02 billion in its IPO on April 24, reflecting institutional appetite for nuclear energy infrastructure investments as energy security concerns intensify amid the Middle East conflict.

news.title: ECB raises 2026 inflation forecast to 2.6 percent; June rate hike now priced in
news.text: The ECB raised its 2026 inflation forecast to 2.6 percent from prior estimates, citing higher energy costs from the Hormuz closure. Financial markets are pricing a 25 basis point hike at the June ECB meeting, with the key rate potentially reaching 2.5 percent by year-end.

news.title: Begona Gomez formally charged April 13 with influence peddling and embezzlement
news.text: Judge Juan Carlos Peinado formally charged Begona Gomez, wife of PM Pedro Sanchez, with influence peddling, embezzlement, and misappropriation of funds related to a university master's degree programme. The Spanish prosecutor has requested acquittal, making this a politically charged contested case.

news.title: Singapore Q1 GDP grows 4.6 percent YoY; MAS tightens SGD NEER slope
news.text: Singapore's economy expanded 4.6 percent year-on-year in Q1 2026, moderating from 5.7 percent in Q4. The MAS on April 14 increased the rate of appreciation of the Singapore dollar NEER policy band in response to inflation pressures from elevated global energy costs, reinforcing SGD strength.

news.title: Consumer sentiment hits record low 47.6 as energy costs bite US households
news.text: The University of Michigan consumer sentiment index hit 47.6, a record low, as rising electricity prices and fuel costs weigh on household confidence. Energy costs are emerging as an unusual political force ahead of US midterm elections.

news.title: Jet fuel crisis forces European airline flight cancellations
news.text: Rising jet fuel costs from the Hormuz-driven crude surge are forcing European airlines to cancel tens of thousands of flights, with energy authorities warning of possible regional jet fuel shortages if supply routes are not replenished soon.

day_ahead: Key market events week of April 27: Mag-7 earnings avalanche April 29-30 (Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft, Meta, Apple). US PCE inflation data for March. Fed officials speaking throughout the week. ECB speakers post-inflation forecast revision. Singapore SGX reopens Monday. Watch oil for any Hormuz ceasefire breakthrough or breakdown.

ai_news.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 with enhanced coding and computer use â€” April 23
ai_news.text: OpenAI launched GPT-5.5 on April 23, rolling out to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise subscribers. The model features improved coding, enhanced computer use, and deeper research abilities, positioning OpenAI toward its super app vision.

ai_news.title: DeepSeek unveils V4 Flash and V4 Pro â€” claims most powerful open-source platform â€” April 24
ai_news.text: China's DeepSeek announced preview versions of its V4 model series on April 24, citing top-tier performance on coding benchmarks and major advances in reasoning and agentic tasks, one year after its disruptive V3 breakthrough.

ai_news.title: Google releases new AI agents and Ironwood TPU; Anthropic commits to 1 million chips â€” April 22
ai_news.text: At Google Cloud Next on April 22, Google unveiled new agentic AI systems and its seventh-generation Ironwood TPU at 4.6 petaFLOPS per chip. Anthropic committed to purchasing up to one million Ironwood units, underscoring the scale of the AI infrastructure arms race.

ai_news.title: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab signs multi-billion dollar Google Cloud deal â€” April 22
ai_news.text: Thinking Machines Lab agreed to a single-digit billion-dollar deal with Google Cloud on April 22 to access Google AI systems built on Nvidia GB300 chips, expanding rapidly just months after its launch.

ai_news.title: Anthropic and NEC partner to build Japan's largest AI engineering workforce â€” April 24
ai_news.text: Anthropic announced a strategic partnership with NEC on April 24 to develop Japan's largest AI engineering workforce, marking a significant expansion of Anthropic's Asia-Pacific footprint and enterprise market localisation strategy.
